fr.recommendations: Enhancing emergency responders' effectiveness on the property could be supported by several actions. Improving the delineation and maintenance of the pedestrian walkways may facilitate easier movement and navigation for personnel on foot. Trimming or strategically managing dense vegetation around key entry points and along pathways could enhance visibility and create clearer access routes. Considering an additional access point might provide alternative entry for responders, minimizing delays and providing greater flexibility in response tactics. Ensuring that the front driveway remains unobstructed and clearly marked at all times could further assist in expedient access and accommodate emergency vehicles efficiently. Finally, the integration of identifiable markers and clear signage at strategic locations may improve navigation across the property, particularly around the main building and roof system areas.

wildfire.recommendations: Enhancing the separation between vegetation and the structure by trimming or removing any adjacent vegetation could improve defensible space. Reducing the density of the tree cluster and scattered trees, as well as managing the dense tree canopy, would help to decrease fuel continuity and create strategic breaks that could limit fire spread potential. It is important to address the vegetation overhang to prevent potential pathways for fire. Maintenance of the vegetated perimeter should aim to preserve defensible space areas around the property. Increasing defensible space, especially if currently limited or fragmented, could enhance the property's resilience. Furthermore, clearing or reducing vegetation adjacent to the access route may support improved firefighter access and suppression efforts, ultimately bolstering the long-term wildfire readiness and adaptability of the property.
